features.description: CRITICAL: Redesign the default OG image for maximum shareability. The current app/opengraph-image.tsx generates 4 colored dots and generic text — this is what shows when someone shares lennys-greatest-hits.vercel.app on LinkedIn/X. It looks like a placeholder. Nobody clicks it. FIX: Redesign to be visually striking. Use ImageResponse with: (1) Dark background (#0a0a0a) with subtle warm gradient. (2) Large bold title 'Lenny's Greatest Hits' (64px). (3) Tagline: 'Lenny's best podcast insights — turned into real, playable songs' (24px, muted). (4) Stats: '102 songs · 11 albums · 5 hr 32 min' (18px). (5) A visual grid of 6 album-colored squares (each album's accent color) arranged in a 3x2 grid to suggest album art. (6) Small footer: 'lennys-greatest-hits.vercel.app'. Make it look like a Spotify Wrapped card — dark, bold, colorful. DONE: OG image is visually compelling. DO NOT TOUCH: track/album-specific OG images in app/track and app/album dirs. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

CRITICAL: Improve per-track OG images. The app/api/og/route.tsx generates OG images for tracks and albums, but it's text-only — no visual hook. When someone shares a specific song on X/LinkedIn, the preview should make people want to click. FIX: For track OG images (renderTrackOG function): (1) Add a large album accent color block/stripe on the left side (80px wide, full height). (2) Make the track title HUGE (64px, bold white). (3) Show the guest name prominently: 'Based on [guest]'s episode with Lenny' (22px). (4) Show the keyQuote in italics if it exists. (5) Bottom: 'lennys-greatest-hits.vercel.app · Hit Play'. For album OG images: (1) Show the album title huge (72px). (2) Show subtitle. (3) Show track count. (4) Accent color stripe. Make both look like premium music app share cards. DONE: Track and album OG images are visually compelling. DO NOT TOUCH: the edge runtime or imports (keep it under 1MB). VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Add sitemap.xml and robots.txt for basic discoverability. Even though SEO isn't the priority, having a sitemap helps social platforms crawl and cache OG images faster. FIX: (1) Create app/sitemap.ts that generates a sitemap with all track URLs, album URLs, and the homepage. Use Next.js built-in sitemap generation (export default function sitemap()). (2) Create app/robots.ts that allows all crawlers. Both are simple server-side files. DONE: /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt are accessible. DO NOT TOUCH: any client components. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Improve share text across all share points. Current share text is generic. FIX: (1) In app/track/[slug]/TrackPageClient.tsx: shareText should be `"[Track Title] — [Guest]'s insights from Lenny's podcast, turned into a song 🎵 [url]"`. If no guest, use `"[Track Title] — startup wisdom turned into music you can actually feel 🎵"`. (2) In app/album/[slug]/AlbumPageClient.tsx: album share text should be `"[Album Title]: [track count] songs about [subtitle]. Built from Lenny's podcast archive 🎵"`. (3) In components/NowPlayingBar.tsx: the copy-link share currently just copies the URL. After copying, the tooltip should say 'Link copied! Share it with your team'. (4) Check all handleShareX functions — make sure the X share text is compelling, not just the title. DONE: All share text is compelling and explains what LGH is. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Add structured data (JSON-LD) for tracks and albums. This helps social platforms and search engines understand the content type. FIX: (1) In app/track/[slug]/page.tsx (the server component), add a JSON-LD script tag with MusicRecording schema: { '@type': 'MusicRecording', name, byArtist: 'Lenny\'s Greatest Hits', inAlbum, duration, url }. (2) In app/album/[slug]/page.tsx, add MusicAlbum schema: { '@type': 'MusicAlbum', name, numTracks, track: [...] }. (3) Check if app/layout.tsx already has a WebApplication schema — if so, update it with the correct track count. DONE: Structured data on all pages. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Add proper meta titles and descriptions to all pages. FIX: (1) In app/track/[slug]/page.tsx: generateMetadata should return { title: '[Track] — [Guest] | Lenny\'s Greatest Hits', description: '[concept]. Based on [source title].' }. Check if this already exists — if so, verify the description is compelling, not just the concept field. (2) In app/album/[slug]/page.tsx: { title: '[Album] | Lenny\'s Greatest Hits', description: '[album description]' }. (3) In app/songs/page.tsx: add metadata { title: 'All Songs | Lenny\'s Greatest Hits', description: 'Browse all [N] songs...' }. (4) In app/about/page.tsx: add metadata. DONE: Every page has a unique, compelling title and description. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Content accuracy: spot-check 10 tracks across all 11 albums. Use mcp__lennysdata__search_content to verify for each: (1) guest appeared on Lenny's podcast/newsletter, (2) keyQuote is real or reasonable, (3) source URL looks valid. Pick 1 track per album (some albums have 15+ tracks, just pick one). Write content/qa/round8-accuracy-audit.md. DONE: Audit exists. DO NOT TOUCH: code. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
Fix accuracy issues from Round 8 spot-check. Read content/qa/round8-accuracy-audit.md. Fix any wrong attributions, quotes, or URLs. Use Lenny MCP to verify. DONE: Fixed. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Add Twitter card meta tags explicitly. Next.js may auto-generate some, but verify each page has: twitter:card = 'summary_large_image', twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image. In app/layout.tsx metadata, ensure the twitter object has card: 'summary_large_image'. For track/album pages, the generateMetadata should include twitter-specific fields that reference the OG image API. DONE: Twitter cards show large image previews. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.

Add canonical URLs to prevent duplicate content. FIX: In app/layout.tsx metadata, add metadataBase (may already exist) and ensure alternates.canonical is set on track and album pages. This tells search engines and social platforms the definitive URL. DONE: Canonical URLs set. VERIFY: pnpm build passes.
